east-village.note: Street parking is a knockdown/theft gamble with brutal alternate-side churn, but Rising Wolf (E 9th St) is a moto-only garage with 24h keyed access; Manhattan moto rates run $150-275/mo (MAD Transpo NYC guide + risingwolfgarage.com, Jun 2026).

lower-east-side.note: Narrow nightlife-heavy streets mean tip-overs and theft risk overnight; no dedicated moto garage found so figure the Manhattan $150-275/mo band (MAD Transpo NYC guide, Jun 2026) - upside is the Williamsburg Bridge on your doorstep for BQE/Rockaways escapes.

williamsburg.note: Real moto community: MotorGrrl on Metropolitan runs $100-175/mo and Works Engineering on N 12th ~$125/mo (NYMSTF/forum listings, Jun 2026); side-street parking is workable but nightlife blocks carry vandalism risk.

east-williamsburg.note: Industrial blocks = low alternate-side competition and easy daytime street parking, with MotorGrrl/Brooklyn Moto nearby in the Brooklyn $100-200/mo band (MAD Transpo NYC guide, Jun 2026); desolate blocks raise overnight theft risk, so garage it anyway.

greenpoint.note: Quieter residential streets are kinder to a parked bike than anywhere in Manhattan; Brooklyn garages run $100-200/mo (MAD Transpo NYC guide, Jun 2026), and McGuinness Blvd puts you on the Pulaski/BQE toward the RFK and Palisades fast.

bushwick.note: Industrial stretches make street parking easy, but theft/vandalism signal is the highest of the Brooklyn set - budget the low end of the Brooklyn $100-200/mo garage band for secure storage (MAD Transpo NYC guide, Jun 2026).

astoria.note: Driveways and semi-detached blocks mean genuinely viable street parking; outdoor moto lots from $95/mo and Queens garages $100-175/mo (Yelp/MAD Transpo NYC, Jun 2026), and the RFK Bridge gives the best weekend launch toward Bear Mountain via the Palisades.

long-island-city.note: Tower-district street parking is competitive but many luxury buildings have garages that take bikes; Queens moto rates run $100-175/mo, building garages trend higher (MAD Transpo NYC guide, Jun 2026) - Queensboro and RFK bridges are minutes away.

hells-kitchen.note: Worst street parking of the ten - commercial curbs, theft risk, relentless alternate-side - but 555 W 57th has a dedicated moto floor and Midtown moto rates run ~$170-200/mo (Yelp/NYMSTF, Jun 2026); Lincoln Tunnel to the Palisades Pkwy is the fastest Bear Mountain run in the city.

yorkville.note: Heavy alternate-side competition but calmer than Midtown and the UES is garage-rich; a 59th St garage lists moto parking at $170/mo, Manhattan band $150-275/mo (Yelp/MAD Transpo NYC, Jun 2026), with the FDR-to-RFK ramp making northbound weekend escapes easy.

harlem.note: Residential brownstone blocks beat Midtown for street parking, though alternate-side still bites and 125th St garages charge car rates (a W 125th listing runs $415/mo; moto, figure the Manhattan $150-275 band - SpotAngels/MAD Transpo NYC, Jun 2026); the killer feature is Harlem River Drive to the GWB in ~12 min - the fastest Palisades Pkwy/Bear Mountain launch on this list.

ridgewood.note: Attached-garage and driveway blocks make street parking Astoria-tier easy, with private garage/driveway listings around $120/mo on Spacer and Queens moto spots from ~$150 (Spacer/Craigslist, Jun 2026); the Jackie Robinson Pkwy at Highland Park gets you to the Belt and the Rockaways, but Bear Mountain runs mean crossing the whole city first.

crown-heights.note: Brownstone blocks with classic alternate-side churn - workable but you will circle; the Sportsmen Speed Club on the Bed-Stuy line is a $325/mo moto clubhouse with lifts and tools, while plain Brooklyn garage spots run $100-200/mo (Craigslist/MAD Transpo NYC, Jun 2026) - Eastern Pkwy feeds the Jackie Robinson for ride-outs east.

prospect-heights.note: Tightest curbs of the brownstone-Brooklyn set - small footprint plus Barclays Center event nights and museum traffic; the Atlantic Yards-era towers have garages trending above the Brooklyn $100-200/mo band (MAD Transpo NYC guide, Jun 2026), and Flatbush Ave runs you onto the Manhattan Bridge north or clear down to the Marine Pkwy Bridge and the Rockaways south.

fort-greene.note: Brownstone alternate-side like its neighbors, but the BQE on-ramp at Park Ave sits right on the neighborhood edge - quickest highway access in brownstone Brooklyn; no moto-only garage in Fort Greene/Clinton Hill itself, so figure the Brooklyn $100-200/mo band or Brooklyn Motor Works in Red Hook at ~$120/mo a short ride away (MotoNYC/MAD Transpo NYC, Jun 2026).

bed-stuy.note: Easiest street parking of the brownstone set - wider blocks, less curb competition - though theft risk argues for the Sportsmen Speed Club ($325/mo club garage on the Crown Heights line) or a plain Brooklyn $100-200/mo spot (Craigslist/MAD Transpo NYC, Jun 2026); Broadway to the Williamsburg Bridge or Atlantic Ave east to the Jackie Robinson for weekend ride-outs.

chelsea.note: Hell's Kitchen-grade street parking misery - commercial curbs, gallery-district truck traffic, theft - and the priciest garage band here: Spacer lists Chelsea moto storage at $187.50-312.50/mo (Spacer, Jun 2026); the saving grace is the Lincoln Tunnel a short ride north for the Palisades Pkwy Bear Mountain run.

west-village.note: Narrow crooked streets, zero spare curb, and relentless alternate-side tie it with Chelsea for worst street parking on the list; no moto garage in-neighborhood - Rising Wolf (East Village) and Ryders Alley (downtown) run ~$200/mo while local car garages start north of $300 (NYMSTF/MotoNYC/SpotAngels, Jun 2026) - and the West Side Hwy straight up to the GWB is your ride-out.
